title: Biodiversity Levels Pyramid
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive three-tier pyramid showing genetic, species, and ecosystem diversity with clickable real-world examples

learningObjectives: Classify the three levels of biodiversity and give examples of each
Explain how genetic, species, and ecosystem diversity are nested within each other
Provide real-world examples of each biodiversity level
Describe why all three levels are important for ecosystem resilience
prerequisites: Basic understanding of genes and DNA
Knowledge of what a species is
Familiarity with the concept of an ecosystem

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Visual hierarchy with interactive drill-down examples
keyQuestions: Why is genetic diversity considered the foundation of all biodiversity?
How does losing one level of biodiversity affect the other two?
Can you have high species diversity but low genetic diversity? What are the consequences?
Why do conservationists need to protect all three levels, not just species?
commonMisconceptions: Biodiversity only means the number of species (it also includes genetic and ecosystem diversity)
All members of a species are genetically identical (genetic variation within species is crucial for adaptation)
Ecosystem diversity is less important than species diversity (losing entire ecosystem types has cascading effects)
assessmentOpportunities: Have students classify examples into the correct biodiversity level
Ask students to explain why a monoculture crop field has low genetic diversity and what risks this creates
Challenge students to identify all three levels of biodiversity in their local environment
